title: Talklines/Warmlines - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Talklines/Warmlines 14 Matching Service Providers 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details 211INFO 211INFO RESOURCE PARENT SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support, resource referral, and additional after-hours support for foster parents. Eligibility: Any foster parent with an ODHS Child Welfare case or in the Oregon Foster Care System Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 211 Visit Website More Details AARP OREGON AARP HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a caregiver helpline for people taking care of loved ones. Does not provide advice, but can suggest resources on a variety of topics. 1455 SW Broadway Suite 1490 Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Caregivers of family members Hours: Monday-Friday 5am-5pm Email (877) 335-5885 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AARP OREGON AARP CAREGIVING COMMUNITY RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information connecting caregivers with direct services and support and offers a variety of resources, including care guides, how-to videos, tip sheets, and webinars. Also offers a community resource finder for family caregivers, people living with Alzheimer's and other dementias, and those involved in care-related decisions, including veterans, service members, and their families. AARP provides a web-based or printed caregiver guide with information, materials, and resources for those caring for a loved one. 1455 SW Broadway Suite 1490 Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Paid or unpaid caregivers Hours: Monday-Friday 5am-5pm Email (888) 885-0013 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ALL-OPTIONS ALL-OPTIONS TALKLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a confidential space to talk about pregnancy, parenting, abortion, and adoption. Peer counselors offer judgment-free support at any point before, during, and after pregnancy, regardless of which options the caller is considering. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 7am-10pm, Saturday-Sunday 7am-3pm Email (888) 493-0092 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BABY BLUES CONNECTION PREGNANCY AND POSTPARTUM SUPPORT RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health support for parents and birthing people coping with perinatal mood and anxiety disorders through connection, information and resources. Services include 24-hour phone support, parent to parent support groups online and in-person and partner support for family members who want to support someone coping with postpartum depression or anxiety. Resource packets may be emailed or mailed at no cost. PO Box 33128 Portland, OR 97292 Eligibility: Parents experiencing any kind of perinatal mood disorders and stress related to welcoming a new child, and their family members. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 557-8375 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS HEPPNER BEHAVIORAL HEALTH OREGON WARMLINE / LINEA DE ESPERANZA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a telephone peer support service. Trained peers have both mental health and addiction recovery experience to emotionally support callers. All calls are confidential. 550 West Sperry Street Heppner, OR 97836 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 698-2392 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DEQH HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides culturally-specific peer support helpline for South Asian LGBTQ2+ individuals. Eligibility: Individuals who identify as LGBTQ2+ or questioning and part of the South Asian community, including Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Burma, India, the Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, and Tibet Hours: Thursday/Sunday 5pm-7pm Email (908) 367-3374 Visit Website More Details HELP-4-HEP HELP4HEP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides phone-based peer support to individuals diagnosed with or exposed to hepatitis C. Support includes: help finding resources such as free or low-cost testing and treatment; financial assistance for medical care; and general information on prevention, risk factors, and additional resources. Also provides information via website and app. 1351 Cooper Road Scotch Plains, NJ 07076 Eligibility: Anyone who has been exposed to or diagnosed with the hepatitis C virus and anyone who has questions about any aspect of hepatitis C Hours: Monday-Friday 6am-6pm. Closed federal holidays. Email (877) 435-7443 Get Directions More Details LINES FOR LIFE SENIOR LONELINESS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing loneliness, isolation, depression, or anxiety. Provides active listening, emotional and grief support, information and referrals, and suicide and crisis intervention. 5100 SW Macadam Avenue Suite 400 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Age 55 and older Hours: 7 days per week 5:30am-11:30pm Email (800) 282-7035 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINES FOR LIFE CONSTRUCTION CARELINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a mental health oriented confidential warmline. 5100 SW Macadam Avenue Suite 400 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Individuals who are employed in the construction industry Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 433-7878 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS OF OREGON FAMILY CAREGIVER HELPLINES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a confidential caregiver helpline service providing caregiver-led support, tools and strategies, guidance, and connection to resources. 147 SE 102nd Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 7am-7pm Email (800) 950-6264 4 Get Directions Visit Website More Details POSTPARTUM SUPPORT INTERNATIONAL POSTPARTUM SUPPORT INTERNATIONAL HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a confidential helpline that offers support and local referrals. Shares online information and support groups focused on the emotional and mental health of childbearing families. The Postpartum Support International website helps users locate specialized support coordinators, crisis hotlines, support groups, and local events by state or county. 6706 SW 54th Avenue Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: Pregnant and postpartum individuals, parents, and family members Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 944-4773 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RECOVERY OUTREACH COMMUNITY CENTER SALEM PEER SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers peer support for mental health, addiction, trauma and stress. Provides one-on-one support, support groups, and phones that are available seven days a week for texting or calling. There are eighteen different groups per week. 1190 Broadway Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 18 and older Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Saturday/Sunday 11am-4pm Email (971) 332-5676 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RECOVERY OUTREACH COMMUNITY CENTER SOCC PEER SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers peer support for mental health, addiction, trauma and stress. Provides one-on-one support, support groups, and phones that are available seven days a week for texting or calling. There are eighteen different groups per week. 280 NE Santiam Boulevard Mill City, OR 97360 Eligibility: Ages 18 and older Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Saturday/Sunday 11am-4pm Email (971) 732-8668 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RESOLVE NATIONAL INFERTILITY ASSOCIATION INFERTILITY SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A national infertility association that provides online and in-person support for individuals seeking peer-led and professional-led support groups. 1660 International Drive Suite 600 McLean, VA 22102 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email (703) 556-7172 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WELLMAMA MATERNAL MENTAL HEALTH AND SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support, support groups, and wellness services to people affected by or at risk for mental health conditions related to parenthood. Support people, including partners and babies under age 1, are welcome at all meetings. 576 Olive Street Suite 205 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Families struggling with emotions related to reproductive health, such as pregnancy, the first few years postpartum, and perinatal loss Hours: Support group times vary Email (541) 525-0495 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AARP OREGON AARP HELPLINE BABY BLUES CONNECTION PREGNANCY AND POSTPARTUM SUPPORT RESOURCES COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS OREGON WARMLINE / LINEA DE ESPERANZA LINES FOR LIFE SENIOR LONELINESS LINE NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS OF OREGON FAMILY CAREGIVER HELPLINES POSTPARTUM SUPPORT INTERNATIONAL POSTPARTUM SUPPORT INTERNATIONAL HELPLINE RECOVERY OUTREACH COMMUNITY CENTER PEER SUPPORT RECOVERY OUTREACH COMMUNITY CENTER PEER SUPPORT WELLMAMA MATERNAL MENTAL HEALTH AND SUPPORT SERVICES
